§6Memory Card§f:
The §6Memory Card §f(appliedenergistics2:memory_card) is a §6settings storage device §f that §6saves §f the configuration of an AE2 block. It is the §6primary way §f to copy and paste §6block settings §f between machines.

§6Production Path (tools/network_memory_card.json)§f:
Pattern "abb / cdc" (2x3 pattern):
- §ba§f: §6Calculation Processor §f(appliedenergistics2:material:23) — 1 (top left)
- §bb§f: §6Iron Ingot §f(ingotIron) — 2 (top middle, top right)
- §cc§f: §6Gold Ingot §f(ingotGold) — 2 (middle left, middle right)
- §cd§f: §6Redstone Dust §f(dustRedstone) — 1 (centre)
- Yields 1× Memory Card

§6Function§f:
- §bSettings backup §f: right-click a §6configured §fAE2 block to copy its settings to the Memory Card
- §bSettings restore §f: right-click a §6different §fAE2 block of the same type to paste the settings
- §bStored settings §f: §b1 §fsettings snapshot per card
- §bCompatible blocks §f: ME Controller, ME Drive, ME Chest, ME Interface, ME Storage Bus, ME Import Bus, ME Export Bus, ME Level Emitter, ME Storage Monitor, ME Conversion Monitor, Crafting CPU, ME Security Terminal
- §bCard durability §f: §b100 §f uses (each right-click counts)

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 0 items the Memory Card is a component of, but it is referenced in 8 §6use sites §f across the mod pack. Common uses:
- §bMass configuration §f: copy settings from one §6Storage Bus §f to all others
- §bNetwork backup §f: save the §6Controller §f settings before a §6major §f change
- §bTemplate sharing §f: trade §6Level Emitter §f configurations between players

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: dedicate one §6Memory Card §f per block type (Storage Bus, Level Emitter, etc.) to keep settings organized
- §eTip§f: pair with a §6Biometric Card §f for §6player-restricted §f configuration
- §cWarning§f: the Memory Card §cdestroys §f its previous settings on copy — always keep a §6backup §f
- §cWarning§f: the Memory Card §ccannot §f copy the §bnetwork ID §f; use a §6Security Terminal §f for that
